title: The Driving Instructor
learningGoal: The learner will verbally construct permissive (can), advisory (should), and obligatory (must) statements in real-time, rigorously maintaining the bare infinitive structure without L1 'to' interference.
instructions: You are a driving instructor. You are teaching your friend Amit how to drive a car. Reply ALOUD to his questions. Use Can for permission, Should for advice, and Must for strict traffic rules! And remember the Golden Rule: NEVER use 'to' after a modal!

contentItems.text: If Amit says 'The light is red', tell him a RULE. If he says 'I am tired', give him ADVICE.

scenario: Teaching a friend how to drive.

subTasks.prompt: अमित: 'Can I go now? The light is red.' (क्या मैं अब जा सकता हूँ? बत्ती लाल है।) जवाब दें: 'नहीं! बत्ती लाल है। तुम्हें (ज़रूर) रुकना चाहिए।' (No! The light is red. You must...)

sampleResponse.targetText: No! The light is red. You must stop.
sampleResponse.transliteration: नो! द लाइट इज़ रेड। यू मस्ट स्टॉप।
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: नहीं! बत्ती लाल है। तुम्हें (ज़रूर) रुकना चाहिए।

subTasks.prompt: अमित: 'Okay, I stopped. The light is green now.' (ठीक है, मैं रुक गया। अब बत्ती हरी है।) जवाब दें: 'तुम अब जा सकते हो।' (You can...)
अमित: 'This road is wet. It is raining.' (यह सड़क गीली है। बारिश हो रही है।) जवाब दें: 'तुम्हें धीरे चलाना चाहिए।' (You should...)
अमित: 'I am very tired. Should I drink coffee?' (मैं बहुत थका हुआ हूँ। क्या मुझे कॉफी पीनी चाहिए?) जवाब दें: 'नहीं। तुम्हें आराम करना चाहिए।' (No. You should...)
अमित: 'Okay, I will park the car.' (ठीक है, मैं कार पार्क करूँगा।) जवाब दें: 'तुम कार यहाँ नहीं खड़ी कर सकते। यह एक अस्पताल है!' (You cannot...)

sampleResponse.targetText: You cannot park here! It is a hospital.
sampleResponse.transliteration: यू कैनॉट पार्क हियर! इट इज़ अ हॉस्पिटल।
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: तुम कार यहाँ नहीं खड़ी कर सकते। यह एक अस्पताल है!

scenarioContext.targetText: Can I go now? The light is red.
scenarioContext.transliteration: कैन आई गो नाउ? द लाइट इज़ रेड।
scenarioContext.baseTranslation: क्या मैं अब जा सकता हूँ? बत्ती लाल है।

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: No! The light is red. You must stop.
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: नो! द लाइट इज़ रेड। यू मस्ट स्टॉप।
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: नहीं! बत्ती लाल है। तुम्हें रुकना ही होगा।

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: You cannot park here! It is a hospital.
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: यू कैनॉट पार्क हियर! इट इज़ अ हॉस्पिटल।
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: तुम यहाँ कार खड़ी नहीं कर सकते! यह एक अस्पताल है।

scoringRubric.Zero Tolerance for 'To': Did the user aggressively eliminate 'to' after the modals (e.g. failing if they said 'You must to stop')?
scoringRubric.Semantic Accuracy: Did the user correctly use Must for the red light, and Should for the wet road?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: No, you must stop! You can go now. You should drive slowly. No, you should rest. You cannot park here, it is a hospital!

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You are a great driving instructor. You knew exactly when to give polite advice (Should) and when to enforce absolute laws (Must). All of your verbs were perfectly clean!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep practicing! A very common mistake is saying 'You must to stop'. If you put 'to' inside the sentence, it is completely wrong! Modals must touch the action verb directly: 'You must stop'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you don't confuse 'Should' and 'Must'. Running a red light is a crime, so you MUST say 'Must'. Being tired is just bad, so it is 'Should'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Amit: Can I go now? The light is red...

hintText: Speak with authority! But remember, 'Should' has a silent L. Do not say Sh-u-L-d. Shrink it down to 'Shud'.
commonMistakes: Saying 'You must parked here' (adding an -ed suffix violation).
